Growth Consulting Pricing

Growth Consulting Pricingwithout the guesswork.

Fixed scope at each tier. You see what is included before you sign anything.

Get Growth Consulting PricingGrowth Consulting Services
At a Glance

Most growing businesses hit a ceiling where adding more channels, more content, or more ad spend stops moving the needle. The bottleneck isn't effort. It's alignment. Marketing, sales, and product are optimizing for different things, and no one has a clear model for how the business actually acquires and retains customers. Consulting engagements are separate from execution services. We can manage implementation or advise your internal team.

Investment guidance

Pricing based on engagement scope and advisory cadence.

Entry level

Starter

Best for: Monthly advisory for early-stage businesses. Growth model, channel strategy, and bi-weekly calls.

  • Business & Marketing Audit

    Core

  • Growth Model

    Core

  • Channel Strategy

    Core

  • KPI Framework

    Core

Starting investment

$3,000/mo

Recommended starting point

Growth

Best for: Embedded fractional CMO engagement with full strategy development and execution oversight.

  • Business & Marketing Audit

    Standard

  • Growth Model

    Standard

  • Channel Strategy

    Standard

  • KPI Framework

    Standard

Starting investment

$6,000/mo

Scale tier

Scale

Best for: Senior strategic partnership for growth-stage companies with multi-channel execution and board reporting.

  • Business & Marketing Audit

    Advanced

  • Growth Model

    Advanced

  • Channel Strategy

    Advanced

  • KPI Framework

    Advanced

Starting investment

$10,000+/mo

Consulting engagements are separate from execution services. We can manage implementation or advise your internal team.

What's included

Strategic advisory from business audit through execution roadmap and ongoing guidance.

Scope of work

  • Business & Marketing Audit

    Full review of current channels, unit economics, funnel performance, and growth blockers.

  • Growth Model

    A clear model showing how customers are acquired, retained, and expanded, with levers by stage.

  • Channel Strategy

    Prioritized channel recommendations based on your business model, audience, and available resources.

  • KPI Framework

    The metrics that actually matter for your stage of growth, with targets and accountability structure.

  • Execution Roadmap

    90-day and 12-month plans with clear priorities, owners, and resource requirements.

  • Monthly Advisory Sessions

    Regular strategy sessions to review performance, adjust priorities, and unblock execution.

Common Questions

FAQ

Is there a setup fee?+

Most engagements include onboarding and strategy in month one. We quote it upfront. No surprise charges on the first invoice.

Do you require a long-term contract?+

No. Month to month. Results should earn your business, not a contract clause.

What affects my price?+

Consulting engagements are separate from execution services. We can manage implementation or advise your internal team.

Can I start with a smaller plan and upgrade?+

Yes. Many clients start on Starter and move up once momentum builds. We plan the upgrade path so you are not rebuilding from scratch.

How is growth consulting different from your other services?+

Growth consulting is strategy and planning. Our other services are execution. Many clients start here to build the roadmap, then engage us or their internal team for SEO, ads, web design, and other channels.

Do you execute the strategy or just advise?+

Both. Advisory engagements give your team the plan and quarterly guidance. Managed engagements add our execution team running the channels in the roadmap.

How much does growth consulting cost?+

Advisory retainers run $3,000 to $6,000 per month. Project-based strategy deliverables range from $5,000 to $15,000 for the audit, competitive analysis, and roadmap document.

How long does a growth consulting engagement last?+

Most advisory clients work with us 3 to 6 months to build and refine the plan, then move to execution. Some keep us on quarterly for strategic oversight.
